# React: useReducer and State Patterns (2026-06-14)

## useReducer for Complex State

When state transitions are complex, `useReducer` is cleaner:

```jsx
const initialState = { count: 0, loading: false };

function reducer(state, action) {
  switch (action.type) {
    case 'increment':
      return { ...state, count: state.count + 1 };
    case 'decrement':
      return { ...state, count: state.count - 1 };
    case 'set_loading':
      return { ...state, loading: action.payload };
    default:
      return state;
  }
}

const [state, dispatch] = useReducer(reducer, initialState);
```

## Lifting State Up

When siblings need to share state, lift it to the nearest common ancestor:

```
Parent (owns state)
├── ChildA (receives value via props)
└── ChildB (receives setter via props)
```

## useRef for Persistence

Unlike state, updating a ref does NOT trigger re-render:

```jsx
const renderCount = useRef(0);
useEffect(() => {
  renderCount.current += 1;
});
```
